spec_match.rationale: The matrix and recommended catch block have the direction REVERSED: claims pre-135 was NotAllowedError and 135+ is SecurityError. The feature title and chromestatus say the opposite — SecurityError → NotAllowedError. Authors copying this code will misroute their catches and break in production.

interactive.rationale: Three probe buttons (spc, cancel, abort) invoke `credentials.create` for real and render the actual `error.name` into the matrix's third column, plus a rolling log.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Lede promises a comparison of pre/post and a forward-compatible catch — delivered, but with the rename direction wrong.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers SPC, cancel, and abort, but the underlying create call hardcodes `rp.id = 'example.test'` which will fail with `SecurityError` for an unrelated reason (rpId mismatch), polluting the matrix's `this browser` column.

references.rationale: Links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5106102997614592 which is `secure-payment-confirmation-browser-bound-keys`, NOT this feature (5160752715137024). The cleanup script /tmp/fix-slugs.py reads this ID to recover the slug — a wrong ID would mis-slug or delete the folder.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables, matrix uses ok-cell/bad-cell semantic classes, warn-block uses accent-rose, no static cards.

openQuestions.title: Reverse the rename direction across the page
openQuestions.detail: The matrix row `cross-origin SPC, no payment extension consent` says pre-135=NotAllowedError, 135+=SecurityError. Per the feature title and the credential-creation-tester sibling, this is BACKWARDS. The actual change is SecurityError → NotAllowedError. The recommended catch block at the bottom propagates the wrong direction and is the worst-case migration risk. Reverse both the matrix expectations and the catch-block comment.

openQuestions.title: Fix the chromestatus reference ID
openQuestions.detail: Page links chromestatus 5106102997614592 (secure-payment-confirmation-browser-bound-keys). The correct ID is 5160752715137024. The cleanup script reads this ID — a wrong one would silently re-slug or delete the folder.

openQuestions.title: Fix the rpId mismatch that throws SecurityError for an unrelated reason
openQuestions.detail: The probe hardcodes `rp: { name: 'demo.test', id: 'example.test' }`. The browser throws SecurityError because the rpId doesn't match the document origin — not because of the SPC alignment fix. The matrix's `this browser` column therefore reports SecurityError for every scenario regardless of pre/post 135 behaviour. Set `rp.id = location.hostname`.

openQuestions.title: Reproduce in a cross-origin iframe
openQuestions.detail: The original bug only fires in a cross-origin iframe. From the top-level page, both pre-135 and 135+ Chrome throw NotAllowedError. Add an iframe variant that hosts the create call so the matrix's `this browser` column can actually distinguish the version.

summary: This page is structurally clean but its core claim is inverted: it tells authors to expect SecurityError on 135+ when actually they should expect NotAllowedError. Combined with the wrong chromestatus ID and a misconfigured rpId, the page actively misinforms — needs urgent rewrite.
